title: Her Body Is Not My Measure
subtitle: An affirmation for stopping the comparison before it gets its grip
excerpt: A short affirmation for when someone else's body has become the standard yours is being judged against. Her body is not the measure. The affirmation names that clearly and closes the comparison.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 11 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you have caught yourself using another woman's body as the standard: at the gym, scrolling, in a mirror with someone nearby.

content.text: A one-minute affirmation for the comparison. The comparison happens fast and often without noticing. The affirmation names what is actually happening and closes it.
New to affirmations as a practice? Read
The comparison picks a body that is not yours and runs yours against it. It runs quickly, often more quickly than the thinking mind can notice. The affirmation interrupts the loop.
Her body is not my measure. My body is the only measure I am working with.
Say it when the comparison shows up. Say it quietly or in your head if you are in public. Say it twice if once does not close the loop.
Adapted from Day 11 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
